params.text:
Good housekeeping prevents harm

Prevention is mostly about routine, sensible habits rather than expensive equipment. Keeping floors clean and dry, walkways clear and lighting working removes the vast majority of hazards before anyone is hurt.

  • Clean up spillages the moment you notice them
  • Put out a warning sign and leave it until the floor is fully dry
  • Keep corridors, doorways and fire exits clear of clutter
  • Tuck away or cover trailing cables
  • Report failed lights, loose flooring and other defects promptly
  • Wear sensible, well-fitting non-slip footwear

params.alt: Trailing cables are a common trip hazard

params.taskDescription: Complete this good-practice guidance on dealing with a spillage. Drag each word into the correct gap.
params.textField: Clean up a spillage *immediately* and place a warning *sign* by the wet floor. Leave the sign in position until the surface is completely *dry*. If you cannot clear the hazard yourself, *report* it so it can be dealt with. Choosing non-slip *footwear* also reduces your risk of a slip.

params.question: It is fine to leave a spillage and deal with it later if you are busy.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Spillages must be dealt with immediately and marked with a sign, as anyone passing could slip and be injured.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. A spillage should be cleaned up immediately and marked with a warning sign, because anyone could slip in the meantime.
